sensitivity_rules.guidance: Standard warfarin dosing. Typical maintenance dose 5-7 mg/day for most adults. Use standard dose-finding algorithms.
Reduced warfarin dose may be needed (typically 3-5 mg/day). One CYP2C9 variant allele reduces metabolism. Monitor INR closely during initiation.
Reduced warfarin dose recommended (typically 3-5 mg/day). VKORC1 variant increases warfarin sensitivity. More frequent INR monitoring advised.

Significantly reduced warfarin dose needed (typically 1-3 mg/day). Both CYP2C9 and VKORC1 variants present. CPIC recommends 50-70% dose reduction with frequent INR monitoring. Consider pharmacogenomic dosing algorithm.

Significantly reduced warfarin dose needed (typically 0.5-2 mg/day). Homozygous or compound heterozygous CYP2C9 reduced-function variants. Very high bleeding risk. CPIC recommends ≥50% dose reduction with very frequent INR monitoring.
